enums.name: Role
enums.description:
Role is the coarse membership grade. It maps 1:1 onto a relation in the
`workspace` authz namespace, ordered owner ⊃ admin ⊃ member ⊃ guest.

values.name: WORKSPACE_TYPE_PERSONAL
values.description:
PERSONAL is the single auto-provisioned workspace every user owns. It
cannot be deleted and admits exactly one member (the owner).

values.name: WORKSPACE_TYPE_TEAM
values.description:
TEAM is any workspace a user creates to collaborate with others —
a family, a project group, or a whole company.

messages.name: CheckRequest
messages.description:
Check answers "does subject have relation on namespace:object_id?",
evaluating the namespace's userset rewrite rules transitively.

fields.name: workspace_id
fields.description: optional owning workspace; empty for a standalone group.

fields.name: slug
fields.description: slug is optional; the server derives one from display_name when empty.

messages.name: ExpandRequest
messages.description: Expand returns the effective userset tree for namespace:object_id#relation.

fields.name: workspace_id
fields.description:
workspace_id optionally scopes the group to a workspace (a company's
internal groups). Empty for project-level / standalone groups (a B2C
user's "family" list).

messages.name: GroupMember
messages.description: GroupMember is either a user or a nested group.

fields.name: token
fields.description:
token is returned ONLY from CreateInvitation so the caller can deliver
it out of band; it is never echoed by ListInvitations.

fields.name: workspace_id
fields.description: optional: restrict to groups owned by this workspace.

messages.name: ListWorkspacesRequest
messages.description:
ListWorkspaces returns every workspace acting_user_id is an active member
of, auto-provisioning their PERSONAL workspace on first call.

messages.name: ReadRelationTuplesRequest
messages.description:
ReadRelationTuples returns stored tuples matching the non-empty filter
fields (exact match). It does NOT evaluate userset rewrites — it is the
raw store read. Use Check for permission decisions.

fields.name: subject_user_id
fields.description: optional: restrict to tuples whose subject is this concrete user.

messages.name: Subject
messages.description: Subject is the right-hand side of a tuple: a concrete user OR a userset.

messages.name: SubjectSet
messages.description:
SubjectSet references the set of subjects related to another object — the
userset `namespace:object_id#relation`. When relation is empty it denotes
the object itself (rarely needed).

fields.name: user_ids
fields.description: For LEAF nodes: the concrete subjects and usersets at this node.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: children
fields.description: For UNION nodes: the child subtrees.
fields.label: repeated

fields.name: project_id
fields.description:
project_id is the isolation shard (identity ADR-0002): every row and
every relation tuple is scoped to it.

fields.name: project_id
fields.description: project_id scopes every tuple in the batch; empty = default project.

scalarValueTypes.notes: Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint32 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Inefficient for encoding negative numbers – if your field is likely to have negative values, use sint64 instead.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int32s.
Uses variable-length encoding. Signed int value. These more efficiently encode negative numbers than regular int64s.
Always four bytes. More efficient than uint32 if values are often greater than 2^28.
Always eight bytes. More efficient than uint64 if values are often greater than 2^56.
A string must always contain UTF-8 encoded or 7-bit ASCII text.
May contain any arbitrary sequence of bytes.
